title: abc fails to compile on arm64 with conda clang
body:
### Version

---

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

Attempt to build Clang in Conda on Mac for arm64

### Expected Behavior

yosys should build

### Actual Behavior

Aside from a litany of warnings about the use of `sprintf`, compilation appears to fail due to these calls:

```
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:424:31: error: non-constant-expression cannot be narrowed from type 'unsigned int' to 'int' in initializer list [-Wc++11-narrowing]
            int pLitsF[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pOne, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pOne, i+1)->Value };
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:424:31: note: insert an explicit cast to silence this issue
            int pLitsF[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pOne, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pOne, i+1)->Value };
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                              static_cast<int>(        )
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:424:58: error: non-constant-expression cannot be narrowed from type 'unsigned int' to 'int' in initializer list [-Wc++11-narrowing]
            int pLitsF[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pOne, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pOne, i+1)->Value };
                                                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:424:58: note: insert an explicit cast to silence this issue
            int pLitsF[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pOne, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pOne, i+1)->Value };
                                                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                                                         static_cast<int>(          )
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:425:31: error: non-constant-expression cannot be narrowed from type 'unsigned int' to 'int' in initializer list [-Wc++11-narrowing]
            int pLitsS[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pTwo, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pTwo, i+1)->Value };
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
src/base/acb/acbTest.c:425:31: note: insert an explicit cast to silence this issue
            int pLitsS[2] = { Gia_ManCo(pTwo, i)->Value, Gia_ManCo(pTwo, i+1)->Value };
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                              static_cast<int>(        )
```
